seo.title: SavedSouls Foundation — Animal Rescue Thailand
seo.description: We rescue dogs and cats in Thailand — including paralyzed and disabled animals that other shelters turn away. Adopt, donate or volunteer in Khon Kaen, Thailand.

home.headline: Where Broken Souls Learn to Love Again
home.heroSubtitle: They came broken. They leave whole. You make the difference.
home.heroOneliner: 2,500+ souls saved · Wheelchairs · Swim therapy · Love that never gives up
home.intro1: They came to us broken. They leave us whole. Deep in Thailand's northeastern heartland, Saved Souls Foundation is more than a dog shelter—it's where broken souls learn to love again.
home.spotlightTagline: Love doesn't give up—neither do we.
home.spotlightTitle: A new story every week. You write the new beginning.
home.spotlightGiveMeAChance: Will you give me a chance?
home.donateTitle: Your Money. Their Life. Total Transparency.
home.donateSubtitle: Every euro. Every soul. Every heartbeat counts. Choose your impact:
home.donate50: covers a sterilization campaign preventing 100+ unwanted litters
home.donate100: provides a custom wheelchair for a paralyzed pup
home.donate250: gives a dog weeks of swimming therapy—freedom in the water
home.donate500: feeds the entire sanctuary for a week
home.donate5000: Honorary member · Super donor · Great helper – your gift feeds us for months and saves dozens of souls
home.donate10000: Golden honorary member · Huge help · Our hero – you are the rescue for hundreds of animals
home.donateBreakdown: Where every baht goes: 70% direct animal care · 20% sterilization campaigns · 10% shelter maintenance
home.donateImpactGeneric: Every euro contributes to food, medicine and care for our animals
home.donateThanksImageAlt: Thank you – your donation helps rescued dogs and cats in Thailand

home.findOutMoreTitle: Love Doesn't Give Up—Neither Do We
home.findOutMoreText: Through years of patient love, swimming therapy for our paralyzed dogs and disabled dogs, and a promise that every soul matters, we're rewriting their stories. When others see "too damaged," we see deserving. When others say "impossible," we say "not yet." Read how we rescue dogs and care for wheelchair dogs in Thailand.
home.findOutMoreHeroCta: Be part of our Soul Saver community
home.findOutMoreHeroLocation: Animal Shelter in Khon Kaen, Thailand
home.adoptDiversityTitle: Dogs and cats: puppies, healthy, or with special needs
home.adoptDiversityText: Not just disabled dogs. We have healthy dogs, puppies, cats, and animals with special needs—all looking for a loving home.
home.adoptTitle: Your Next Best Friend is Waiting—Wheelchair and All
home.adoptText: Our rescued dogs have survived the unthinkable. They've learned to trust again. Now they're ready for the greatest adventure: coming home to you. Our dog adoption process isn't fast—because these souls deserve perfection, not speed. Because this isn't just adoption. This is redemption.
home.adoptHubText: Every rescued dog is leash-trained, socialized, and prepared. Every adopter is carefully matched, supported, and welcomed into our family. Adopt a dog from Thailand's only shelter for disabled and special needs animals. Because this isn't just adoption. This is redemption.
home.soulWaitingTitle: Right Now, a Soul is Waiting for You
home.soulWaitingText: At this very moment, a paralyzed dog is lying in our clinic—waiting for the wheelchair that will give them freedom again. A meat trade survivor is taking their first tentative steps toward trusting a human hand. Your donation to our dog rescue shelter in Thailand is the difference between survival and surrender.
home.miraclesText: Where other shelters see "hopeless," we see hope. Our mission? Saving stray dogs, rescued dogs, and disabled animals no one else will fight for—in Khon Kaen, Thailand.
home.miraclesCard1Desc: Paralyzed dogs who deserve wheelchairs, not euthanasia. Rescued dogs who need swimming therapy, not sympathy.
home.miraclesCard2Desc: Rescued dogs learning that hands can heal, not harm. Stray dogs who never knew they mattered—until now.
home.miraclesCard2Cta: Read about the dog meat trade →
home.miraclesCard3Desc: Cook fresh meals. Train. Rehabilitate. Love fiercely. Our rescued dogs didn't survive hell just to be forgotten. They survived because they were meant to thrive.
home.miraclesCardCta: Click to see where donations go →
home.everyEuroTitle: Every Euro. Every Soul. Every Heartbeat Counts.
home.everyEuroText: No corporate salaries. No fancy offices. Just you, us, and the animals who need us. Your money becomes their life.
home.statsTimeline: In 2010, we built a shelter for dogs nobody wanted. 2,500+ souls later, we're still building — second chances.
home.sponsorText: Your monthly support funds wheelchairs for paralyzed dogs, swimming therapy for rescued dogs, fresh meals for stray dogs, and the patient love that turns "hopeless" into hope. Sponsor a dog and be the reason a soul thrives.
home.volunteerTitle: Come Volunteer – Transform Your Own Life
home.volunteerText: Want to do more than donate? Come to Thailand. Hug dogs who have never known love. Look into eyes learning to hope again. Many volunteers call it "the most meaningful experience of their lives."
home.quote: We can't save them all. But for each one we save, we change their entire world.
home.video1Title: Meet our rescued dogs at Saved Souls Foundation
home.video2Title: A Sanctuary in the Heart of Isaan
home.video2Text: Deep in Khon Kaen, in the lush northeast of Thailand, a small team with enormous hearts runs a shelter that never turns away a soul. Hundreds of rescued dogs and cats call this place home—and every day, our dedicated staff and volunteers prove that love can heal what the world has broken.
home.contactThanksMessage: Your message has been sent to info@savedsouls-foundation.org. We'll get back to you as soon as we can.
home.contactError: Something went wrong. Please try again.
home.contactCtaSubtext: We'd love to hear from you.
home.testimonialQuote: I visited Saved Souls in 2024. The love and patience they give to each disabled dog is unforgettable. I now sponsor two wheelchairs.

exitPopup.title: ⚠️ Our land is being sold
exitPopup.subtitle: 350 dogs will lose their home — unless we can buy the land. Help them now.

dogFood.title: Monthly Dog Food
dogFood.intro1: With 430 dogs to feed we are always in need of food!
dogFood.intro2: Due to shortages of dry kibbles, we cook food for dogs every day and we use 2 large bags (100 kg) of rice mixed with meat or dry kibbles for the dogs. Our disabled and clinic dogs need special diets and extra food daily to help with their rehabilitation and to make them well again.
dogFood.intro3: We are always in need of rice, dry kibbles, canned dog food and treats.
dogFood.totalCost: Total costs for dog food: 72.000 baht per month
dogFood.cost3: • A box of canned dog food = 800 Baht
dogFood.cost4: Total costs for Rice = 42.000 Baht per month
dogFood.cost5: Total costs for Kibbles = 30.000 Baht per month
dogFood.cta: DONATE NOW — HELP FEED OUR DOGS →

catFood.title: Monthly Cat Food
catFood.intro1: At Saved Souls Foundation we also have a cat run where we take care of 91 cats, which were rescued from the streets or from bad situations. We love our furry friends, but we need your help to keep them fed and healthy. Donating cat food is always appreciated as cat food is expensive and we go through a lot of it every day.
catFood.intro2: We are always in need of tinned cat food, dry kibbles, treats and cat sand.
catFood.totalCost: Total costs for cat food and sand: around 15.000 baht per month
catFood.cost3: Total costs for Canned Food = 6.400 Baht per month
catFood.cost4: Total costs for Kibbles = 5.760 Baht per month
catFood.cost5: Total costs for Cat Sand = 3.000 Baht per month
catFood.cta: DONATE NOW — HELP FEED OUR CATS →

medical.title: Monthly Medical Donation
medical.intro1: Medical Support is what we lack the most, as certain essential medications and operations are expensive. Donors are the only ones who can help us support badly injured dogs and cats and organize sterilization events to avoid new unwanted pregnancies—as we already have too many street animals to save.
medical.intro2: We get daily requests to save badly injured dogs and cats!
medical.intro3: Unfortunately without funds we cannot always help the animals in need to get their surgery done, or they have to wait days or weeks before the surgery—which can alter the outcome.
medical.intro4: Any time, you can become the angel who will ease the pain of a deeply suffering dog or cat by donating to us.
medical.totalCost: Total costs for medical supplies and medication: around 15.000 baht per month
medical.cta: DONATE NOW — BE A MEDICAL ANGEL →

maintenance.title: Monthly Repair & Maintenance Donation
maintenance.sponsorTitle: Be Our Repair and Maintenance Sponsor
maintenance.intro1: Saved Souls Foundation was set up in 2017 and is home for 430 dogs and 91 cats. The foundation consists of multiple areas and runs equipped to take care of our dogs and cats. We also have a dog swimming pool for therapy and a fully equipped clinic as well.
maintenance.intro2: To keep running such a big-size sanctuary and keep it in good shape requires daily maintenance. Not a day passes without the need to fix something broken. When things are not broken, we also often need to improve them. This can be general repairs but also fixing the fences, doors and roofs to keep our animals safe and sound.
maintenance.intro3: Our staff is constantly busy taking care of the dogs, and keeping the sanctuary a safe place for them requires good materials to work with.
maintenance.intro4: We appreciate any amount that can be donated to keep our sanctuary a comfortable and secure home for our numerous animals.
maintenance.totalCost: Total costs for maintenance and repair: around 10.000 baht per month
maintenance.cta: DONATE NOW — HELP MAINTAIN OUR SANCTUARY →

staff.title: Monthly Staff Salary
staff.intro: Our lovely staff take great care of the dogs and cats in our shelter. They work hard every day to give these rescued animals the love and attention they deserve. We couldn't do this without their support. If you want to help our cooks and caretakers, please consider making a donation. Every bit counts!
staff.totalCost: Total staff costs: 120.000 baht per month

donate.comingSoon: More cost breakdowns (wheelchairs, swimming therapy) coming soon.

story.heroSubtitle: The story of Saved Souls Foundation
story.heroIntro: In a small village in northeastern Thailand, hundreds of animals have found safety, trust, and a reason to wag their tail.
story.metaDescription: The story of Saved Souls Foundation: from one woman who couldn't look away to a sanctuary in Ban Kok Gnam, Khon Kaen. Every soul is valuable.
story.intro1: Deep in northeastern Thailand, far from the tourist trails and busy city streets, something quietly extraordinary is taking place.
story.intro2: In a small village called Ban Kok Gnam, in the province of Khon Kaen, hundreds of animals have found something they never expected to find again: safety. Trust. A reason to wag their tail. This is Saved Souls Foundation — and its story begins with one woman who simply could not look away.
story.woman1: Gabriela Leonhard left Switzerland in 2007 in search of a new life. What she found in Thailand she never could have predicted.
story.woman2: Walking the streets of Pattaya, she saw them everywhere — abandoned dogs, abused animals, creatures that had been discarded like they meant nothing. Most people walked past. Gabriela stopped.
story.woman3: She started taking them in. One dog became two. Two became ten. By 2010, she was caring for nearly a hundred animals. She found land in Khon Kaen — 9,600 square meters — and started building. Not just a shelter, but a sanctuary. A place where the forgotten could finally feel found.
story.soundTitle: The Sound She Can Never Forget
story.sound1: Ask Gabriela about the darkest days, and she'll tell you about the trucks.
story.sound2: Every single day, two pickup trucks rolled slowly through the streets. You could hear them coming long before you saw them — a driver's voice crackling over a microphone, announcing that dogs could be exchanged for plastic containers. Household items. A few baht worth of plastic, in exchange for a living animal. The dogs in those trucks were headed for the illegal meat trade.
story.soundQuote: That sound is still deeply rooted in my memory. It was horrifying to witness dogs being traded like this.
story.sound3: Saved Souls began pulling dogs from that trade — first two disabled dogs, then more. In 2013, Thailand officially banned the dog meat trade — a turning point. The country has since introduced an Animal Welfare Act. Progress, hard-won and meaningful.
story.sound4: But the reality on the ground is more complicated. In some villages, dog meat is still consumed. The fight is not over.
story.trust1: The animals that arrive at Saved Souls don't always come wagging their tails. Many have been so deeply traumatised that they have lost all trust in people entirely. Some press themselves into corners. Some flinch at a gentle hand. Some take months before they'll make eye contact.
story.trust2: It can take years of patient, careful socialisation before a dog is ready to trust again. And Saved Souls waits. However long it takes.
story.trust3: Fresh meals are cooked every single day. Swimming therapy sessions help dogs recover — especially those with disabilities, who have found in the water a freedom they couldn't find on land.
story.trust4: Every dog is sterilised and vaccinated. Every animal is treated as what it is: a life that matters.
story.photoGabrielaMelanie: Gabriela and Melanie at Saved Souls Foundation
story.bigger1: Saving individual animals is vital. But Gabriela has always understood that rescue alone cannot solve this problem. That's why Saved Souls runs sterilisation campaigns for street dogs and cats in the surrounding communities. Uncontrolled breeding is at the root of so much suffering — and the only way to truly reduce that suffering is to prevent it at the source.
story.bigger2: The foundation is expanding those programmes and working toward closer collaboration with local government authorities. Disabled dogs receive care and rehabilitation, and once they've recovered, they're matched with adoptive families — given a second chance at life.
story.officialTitle: An Official Foundation, Built on Years of Dedication
story.official1: On October 9th, 2017, Saved Souls became an officially registered non-profit organisation under Thai law — registration number 1/2560. A formal recognition of what had already been true for years.
story.official2: Since 2010, this place has been a lifeline. A last hope. A new beginning.
story.whyQuote: We may not be able to save them all. But for each one we do, their whole world changes.
story.why1: That is the truth at the heart of everything Saved Souls does. Not abstract statistics. Not sweeping gestures. Just one animal at a time, pulled back from the edge — and shown, perhaps for the very first time, that not all humans want to cause harm. Some of us want to heal it.
story.why2: Every soul is valuable. Every single one.
story.chapterTitle: A New Chapter — The Torch is Passed
story.chapter1: Every great story has a moment of transition. For Saved Souls Foundation, that moment has arrived.
story.chapter2: After years of pouring her heart, her energy, and her life into building something truly remarkable, Gabriela Leonhard has announced a new chapter — not an ending, but an evolution.
story.chapter3: Melanie de Wit has stepped in as the new manager of Saved Souls Foundation, taking on one of the most meaningful — and demanding — roles imaginable. Gabriela herself couldn't be more confident in the choice.
story.chapterQuote: Her calm, thoughtful and determined nature will be a great asset to our foundation.
story.chapter4: Gabriela isn't disappearing. She will continue to support Melanie, offer guidance where it's needed, and — as she makes clear — will always have animals around her. That part of who she is will never change.
story.chapter5: What she has built in Ban Kok Gnam doesn't belong to one person. It belongs to the animals who found safety there, the volunteers who showed up, and the supporters around the world who made it possible.
story.chapter6: The mission remains exactly the same. The dedication remains exactly the same. Only the hands carrying it forward have changed — and they are in very good care.
story.chapterClosing: Every soul is valuable. The work continues.

aboutUs.metaDescription: What we do at Saved Souls Foundation: daily care, swimming therapy, wheelchair dogs, sterilization campaigns and adoption. Ban Khok Ngam, Khon Kaen, Thailand.
aboutUs.heroIntro: Daily care, swimming therapy, wheelchair dogs and a safe home for hundreds of animals. This is how we give every soul the attention they deserve.
aboutUs.intro1: Saved Souls Foundation is more than a shelter. It's a place where every animal — disabled or not, old or young — receives the best care we can provide.
aboutUs.intro2: From fresh meals to swimming therapy, from wheelchairs to sterilization campaigns: this is our work.
aboutUs.care1: We are dedicated to providing the highest quality of life for all our animals. Fresh meals are cooked daily.
aboutUs.care2: Swimming therapy plays a crucial role — especially for our disabled dogs, who find in the water a freedom they didn't have on land.
aboutUs.careQuote: Every dog deserves a daily meal and the chance to move. That's not a luxury — it's basic care.
aboutUs.team1: Our dedicated staff and volunteers make sure our dogs and cats receive the attention they need.
aboutUs.team2: From daily walks with as many dogs as possible to bonding time with our cats in the cat shelter — every hand counts.
aboutUs.photoTeam: Team and volunteers with rescued dogs
aboutUs.wheelchair1: For our disabled dogs, we provide wheelchairs and access to a large dog park where they can play, explore and enjoy the fresh air.
aboutUs.wheelchair2: The other dogs are taken for relaxing walks in the peaceful surroundings of our foundation.
aboutUs.loveTitle: Every animal deserves love & a safe home
aboutUs.love1: At Saved Souls Foundation, we believe every animal deserves love, care and a safe place to call home.
aboutUs.love2: Our team makes sure every dog and cat receives the affection and attention they need.
aboutUs.sterilization1: All of our dogs are sterilized and vaccinated.
aboutUs.sterilization2: In addition, we organize sterilization campaigns for street dogs and cats to prevent unwanted litters and reduce suffering in the long term.
aboutUs.adoption1: We help dogs find loving homes both locally and across Thailand.
aboutUs.adoption2: With dedicated care, love and training, we make sure every dog is leash-trained before joining their new family.
aboutUs.adoption3: We also host adoption events where potential owners can meet their future companions in person.
aboutUs.adoption4: Want to join one? Subscribe to our newsletter or follow us on Facebook and Instagram for the latest updates!
aboutUs.visit1: Looking to adopt a dog or cat? You're always welcome to visit our shelter — just contact us to schedule an appointment.
aboutUs.visit3: for general enquiries or fill in the contact form on our homepage.
aboutUs.visit4: You can also reach us by phone:
aboutUs.visit7: Visit us at 133, Ban Khok Ngam, Ban Fang District, Khon Kaen 40270, Thailand.

contactPage.title: Contact
contactPage.subtitle: General enquiries, visits or adoption? Get in touch.
contactPage.openingHours: Opening hours: 1:30 – 3:30 p.m. daily. Please contact us to schedule an appointment.
contactPage.emailLinkBefore: For general enquiries or use the
contactPage.sendMessageSubtitle: Fill out the form below and we'll get back to you as soon as possible.

volunteer.title: Welcome to the furry madness!
volunteer.subtitle: Volunteering that changes your life – Khon Kaen, Thailand
volunteer.intro: Deep in northeastern Thailand, hundreds of dogs and cats are waiting for you. Rescued from the meat trade, from the streets, from abuse. They've lost all trust in humans—until they meet you. Get ready to get sweaty, dirty, and buried in puppy kisses. This is the exhaustion that matters.
volunteer.story1Quote: I worked as a volunteer at Saved Souls. The love and patience they give to each disabled dog are unforgettable. I now sponsor two wheelchairs.
volunteer.story2Quote: Many call it the most meaningful experience of their lives. You look into eyes learning to hope again. You give comfort to paws that once knew only pain.
volunteer.story3Quote: Hundreds of kisses every day. Dogs wagging their tails for the first time again. This isn't a holiday—this is what you live for.
volunteer.story4Quote: I volunteered at Saved Souls Foundation in February and I had the time of my life! It was only 2 weeks but those 2 weeks I will cherish forever. I recommend volunteering here 100%—it is so rewarding. I miss it so much and all the beautiful dogs and staff. I made many friends, and thank you to Sarah who was the volunteer coordinator who went above and beyond for the dogs! As well as management Mon and Gabby. Thank you for having me and I will be back for sure whenever I can.
volunteer.locationText: 40 minutes from Khon Kaen City. Grab a taxi (≈350 baht) or rent a motorbike—and ride into paradise.
volunteer.need5: Minimum commitment: 2 weeks (We invest in training you—let's make it count!)
volunteer.yourDay: A day in the life of a volunteer
volunteer.morningText: Roll up your sleeves! Clean cages, wash bowls, feed 430 dogs and 91 cats. Hard work—but every animal you feed knows you're there.
volunteer.afternoonText: Socialize. Train. Swim with our disabled dogs in the therapy pool. Play. Watch fearful dogs wag their tails for the first time. This is why you came.
volunteer.pack1: Clothes you don't mind getting dirty
volunteer.pack3: Small daypack for water and essentials
volunteer.sharedHouseText: Gender-separated rooms, communal living at its finest.
volunteer.bungalowsText: Available for those wanting their own space.
volunteer.commonAreasText: Enjoy our swimming pool and fully-equipped kitchen.
volunteer.meals2: Cook dinner together with fellow volunteers—we supply the ingredients!
volunteer.perksText: Free WiFi, free washing machines (bring your own detergent)
volunteer.brochureSuffix: for full details, then fill out the contact form.
volunteer.quote: Every soul deserves a hero. Could that be you?
volunteer.footer: Saved Souls Foundation | มูลนิธิเซฟต์ โซลส์
volunteer.footerTagline: Making tails wag, one volunteer at a time. 🐾
volunteer.imgVolunteersAlt: Volunteers with rescued dogs at Saved Souls Foundation sanctuary in Khon Kaen, Thailand
volunteer.imgVolunteerHoldingDogAlt: Volunteer affectionately holding a rescued dog at Saved Souls Foundation
volunteer.imgSwimTherapyAlt: Volunteer with dog in swimming therapy pool at Saved Souls Foundation
volunteer.pdfIntro: We would like to start by thanking you immensely for your interest in volunteering with us to help the hundreds of animals here at Saved Souls Animal Shelter! The help, hard-work and commitment volunteers provide is essential to our cause, and brings hope to the 550+ dogs and 98 cats we have at our shelter.
volunteer.pdfIntro2: Although it can be emotionally challenging to see the injured and sick animals we save, please know that the difference you will make will last forever, both in your heart and theirs.
volunteer.pdfAboutUs: Saved Souls Foundation is a non-profit registered animal charity located in North Eastern Thailand, away from the beaten tracks. It was started in 2010 by Gabriela Leonhard, who, driven by her compassion and love for animals, decided to establish a sanctuary to welcome all unwanted, unloved or abused dogs and cats that would cross her path.
volunteer.pdfAboutUs2: The shelter is currently home to 550+ dogs and about 98 cats. Their origins are diverse – some are local rescues, saved from abusive situations or from the streets after injury or disease. Some are survivors of the illegal dog meat trade who were redirected to Saved Souls after having been saved from trucks by activists. Others were found abandoned at our front door. And many of them were sent to Gabriela by individuals or smaller organisations all around Thailand who had rescued them from the streets, but sadly had no place to shelter them.
volunteer.pdfBelieve: We believe in offering shelter and food to the animals who have no place to go or no one to look after them is a very noble cause. Every new rescue is not "just another dog" on our list, but a frightened soul that needs some food, a warm place to call home and lots of love.
volunteer.pdfBelieve2: However, we know that continuous individual rescues cannot be the long-term answer to the stray overpopulation problem in Thailand. Sterilization is the only long-term solution! At Saved Souls, all animals are sterilized, but we are currently lacking the resources to extend a big sterilization project outside our walls. It is however a goal we are pursuing, and we have started to conduct small projects in the nearby villages.
volunteer.pdfBelieve3: The animal shelter is divided into areas A and B with 75 enclosures. Some of the enclosures are subdivided into lowercase letters. In addition, you will also find a clinic and enclosures for disabled dogs. Don't forget that our animals are looking for a home, so feel free to fall in love and take one (or more) home!
volunteer.pdfReality: Saved Souls Animal Shelter is constantly facing many difficulties and issues. The shelter has become overcrowded, understaffed, with a cruel lack of funding. Its remote location and its poor visibility locally has so far prevented implementation of an efficient adoption program, resulting in a continuous flow of intakes but very few departures.
volunteer.pdfReality2: The staff (8 keepers for over 500 animals) is extremely stretched to perform its basic husbandry duties – cleaning, feeding, basic maintenance, etc., and sadly it means that there is not enough socialization time left for the dogs or for cats, impoverishing their quality of life.
volunteer.pdfReality3: Enclosures and runs are getting old: they need many repairs and improvements (concrete floors to replace the sand base that floods every monsoon season and attracts fleas and ticks, enrichment features in the pens and cages, new roofs offering better cover and light, etc.). We have our small clinic, but equipment and supplies are very limited, restricting us in the level of medical care we would like to provide.
volunteer.pdfSpecialNeeds: The population of Saved Souls Animal Shelter includes about 80 special needs dogs. Most of them were left paralyzed or required limbs to be amputated after road traffic accidents. We are also home to many blind dogs. Saved Souls is one of the rare shelters in Thailand to admit disabled dogs. We believe here that everybody deserves a chance!
volunteer.pdfSpecialNeeds2: Disabled dogs require specific care and attention. They are prone to sores, some need to be expressed, all need to have some exercise time on wheelchairs, physiotherapy or hydrotherapy. At this moment in time, our facilities and arrangements sadly do not permit us to match those daily standard requirements, which is why we need your help! With volunteers on our team, we can have enough staff to make sure our disabled dogs get the attention they need.
volunteer.pdfNeedYou: Saved Souls Animal Shelter is currently in the process of improving its facilities and its daily running in order to offer better lives to rescue animals. And for this... EVERY SOUL IS VALUABLE » WE NEED YOU!
volunteer.pdfAsk1: Do you mind getting dirty? Our daily work involves a lot of grooming, enclosure cleaning, walking on muddy trails etc.
volunteer.pdfAsk2: Are you able to work with very little / zero supervision or guidance after your initial training?
volunteer.pdfAsk3: Are you able to avoid becoming overly distressed when witnessing difficult and emotional situations with injured, sick or disabled animals? Your distress can quickly pass to the animal, causing them more fear and stress. A cool head is key!
volunteer.pdfAsk4: Do you have an open mind? We operate with little funding and may not do things the way you expect.
volunteer.pdfAsk5: Will you respect and accept that the staff are already fully trained in their duties? Suggestions are happily welcomed and we do ask that volunteers come to management for any ideas they have!
volunteer.pdfAsk6: Are you able to work in a hot and humid environment?
volunteer.pdfAsk7: Have you got a deep willingness to help animals to the best of your abilities?
volunteer.pdfWorkNote: Volunteering with animals is hard work that requires commitment. It is not for everyone – loving animals is half the battle, but it's not enough. Even if you have volunteered in different charities before, please understand that the current situation of Saved Souls makes it an even more challenging environment. As a volunteer here you will be required to be very independent, proactive, hard-working, committed and open-minded.
volunteer.pdfWorkNote2: Full training will be provided, and management will always be there for you for any questions, suggestions or concerns, but your schedule for the day will consist of mostly unsupervised work, either alone or with a volunteer for company! This can include feeding a section of our dog enclosures, taking a dog on a walk around the area, or helping to clean an enclosure.
volunteer.pdfHelp1Title: 1. Socialize, socialize, socialize. And then, socialize some more!
volunteer.pdfHelp1: Socialization is very important for the behavioural health of the animals as it ultimately helps them find new homes in loving families. With so many dogs, there are many different temperaments: extremely friendly ones who need love and attention, shy ones who need time to adjust, and extremely timid dogs who remain fearful of humans. For those, we advise spending some quiet, non-interactive time with them in the enclosure, reading a book or just enjoying the scenery. Only a very small percentage of our dogs know how to walk on a lead or obey any commands. Volunteers are encouraged to do some basic training with the dogs to improve their adoptability!
volunteer.pdfHelp2: Our cats have a special room with a small outside area. They are also in need of socialisation and human contact. Our cats can range from extremely friendly to very timid, so socialisation is encouraged!
volunteer.pdfHelp3: We have been lucky to have had many wheelchairs donated so we could provide our paralyzed dogs with the exercise they need, but in practice our staff do not have the time to use them. Volunteers are encouraged to use those wheelchairs to give our special needs dogs some physical exercise! Sessions need to be kept short and fun. Some of our disabled dogs can also benefit immensely from physio or hydro-therapy (we have a special pool and wet suits for our volunteers!). Medical advice need to be solicited from the vet team/management prior any exercise if you are unsure.
volunteer.pdfHelp4Title: 4. Photographers, Youtubers, Videographers, Instagramers - Content Creators
volunteer.pdfHelp4: One of our main goals is improving our visibility online and to do that we need imagery! A LOT of it. We are always looking for high quality content of the shelter and its installations, portraits and videos of the dogs and cats. We encourage you to take pictures during your stay! Please share with the management any material that could benefit the marketing of our shelter. Tag us in your posts! #AnimalShelterThailand #SavedSoulsAnimalShelter #VolunteerinThailand #SavedSoulsFoundation #BeASoulSaver
volunteer.pdfHelp5Title: 5. Keep an eye on the animals
volunteer.pdfHelp5: With so many residents and very few staff, it is unfortunately easy to miss an animal not feeling well, or with an injury. This is one of your most important tasks! Acting as an extra pair of eyes for all of our animals during your daily routine and reporting any modification of behaviour or health issues to the vets or the management is vital to us!
volunteer.pdfGround1: Always use caution when trying to walk a dog on a lead as there are many loose dogs around.
volunteer.pdfGround2: Please be careful with dogs that do not belong to the animal shelter, as they might be aggressive.
volunteer.pdfGround3: Please return the dogs to the correct enclosure. If you're not sure, ask one of our staff members.
volunteer.pdfGround4: Please do not give "rewards" during your runs, as this can lead to jealousy between dogs and start fights.
volunteer.pdfGround5: If a fight begins while you are on a run, clap your hands, use an authoritative voice, stomp your feet, or sprinkle the dogs with water. DO NOT attempt to separate or hit the dogs. If this does not help, call the caretaker.
volunteer.pdfGround6: We advise you to wear long sleeves and long trousers to protect you from scratches.
volunteer.pdfGround7: Despite being regularly treated, you may see dogs with ticks. If you know how to remove them, do so. If not, inform a member of the staff.
volunteer.pdfGround8: Always close the outer door first and then open the inner door. Many of these dogs are escape artists!
volunteer.pdfGround9: If you see an animal that looks sick, please alert a staff member!
volunteer.pdfGround10: You are responsible for the tidiness of your bungalow / volunteer house. At the end of your stay, please leave the premises the way you found them upon arrival.
volunteer.pdfBefore1: We strongly recommend a doctor's visit before coming to Thailand to discuss recommended vaccinations. These may include Typhoid, Hepatitis A and rabies. Consult with your physician.
volunteer.pdfBefore2: You need to have travel insurance. Saved Souls Foundation cannot be held responsible for any accidents, bites or any medical issues that might arise. On arrival you will be asked to sign a waiver of liability.
volunteer.pdfLookingForward: We are looking forward to meet you!
volunteer.sectionTitleWorking: Working as a volunteer at our Saved Souls Shelter
volunteer.sectionTitleHowHelp: How You Can Help During Your Stay

adoptPage.spotlightTitle: A new story every week. You write the new beginning.
adoptPage.luchtbrugLink: ✈️ Adopt worldwide: Europe · Switzerland · USA · Canada · everywhere →
adoptPage.firstPetLink: First time taking a pet home? Read our guide →
adoptPage.happyFaces1Before: These are the happy faces you see in the
adoptPage.happyFaces1After: . Tails wagging. Eyes sparkling. Smiles as big as the sky.
adoptPage.happyFaces2: But behind every happy face is a story.
adoptPage.happyFaces3: We are a small team with a small budget, caring for more than 300 dogs who are completely dependent on us. Many of them only get 5–10 minutes outside once a week because there are simply not enough hands. Some have been here for years. Some were abandoned. Some were found sick or injured. Some have never known what it feels like to belong to one person.
adoptPage.happyFaces4: And yet… look at them. Still trusting. Still loving. Still choosing joy.
adoptPage.happyFaces5: These smiles don't mean they don't need homes.
adoptPage.happyFaces6: They mean they are READY for them.
adoptPage.happyFaces11: You can change a life here.
adoptPage.happyFaces12: Adopt. Sponsor. Donate. Volunteer. Even sharing this website helps more than you know.

adoptPage.pleaseHelpUs: Please help us. With 300+ dogs in our care, they truly need to be adopted. Today, we said goodbye to a 10-year-old dog who sadly spent his final days in the shelter. No dog deserves to end their life without knowing what a real home feels like.

faq.metaTitle: Frequently Asked Questions | Saved Souls Foundation Thailand
faq.metaDescription: Answers to the most common questions about Saved Souls Foundation: donating, adopting, volunteering, and the care of our 300+ dogs and 90+ cats in Khon Kaen, Thailand.
faq.metaKeywords: pet care tips, how to adopt a dog, dog care, dog training, potty train dog, best dog food, raw diet dogs, foods dogs cannot eat, dog vaccination, deworm dog, flea treatment dogs, dog diarrhea, dog vomiting, adopt a cat, best cat food, spay cat cost, cat vaccination
faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Saved Souls Foundation
faq.stillQuestions: Still have questions? We're here to help.

chapters.intro: Discover more about dogs and cats
chapters.introText: From feeding tips to vaccination schedules—our guides help you give your pet the best care. Written by rescuers, for animal lovers.

nutrition.title: Nutrition
nutrition.teaser: Raw or cooked? BARF or kibble? Cut through the confusion. Good food = healthy, happy pet. Discover what to feed—and what to avoid.

health.title: Health
health.teaser: Vaccines save lives. Fleas, ticks, heartworm—arm yourself with knowledge. Your pet deserves the best protection. The complete guide.

firstAid.title: First aid dog and cat
firstAid.teaser: Bleeding, poisoning, choking, overheating—what to do? Plus: what do you need at home? Basic first aid can save lives.

firstPetHome.title: First time dog or cat home
firstPetHome.teaser: Tips for the first days: preparation, rest and patience. A new beginning for you and your furry friend.

behavior.title: Behavior
behavior.teaser: Scared? Reactive? Anxious? Trauma runs deep—but recovery is possible. Learn how we help rescued animals heal, and what you can do too.

training.title: Training & upbringing
training.teaser: Toys, treats, patience. Positive training works—even for animals who've seen the worst. Discover the methods that change lives.

snacks.title: Snacks & chews
snacks.teaser: Rawhide can kill. Here's what's safe—and what isn't. Your pet's safety is worth 2 minutes of reading. Check before you buy.

vaccinations.title: Vaccinations
vaccinations.teaser: When? How often? Why? Vaccination schedules demystified. DHPP, rabies, lepto—knowledge saves lives. Your pet deserves protection.

dogsCats.title: Dogs & cats
dogsCats.teaser: Wheelchair dogs, meat-trade survivors, cats in need—every soul has a story. Discover how we care for them and what they need.

dogHomeAlone.title: Dog home alone
dogHomeAlone.teaser: From barking neighbours to a calm dog: how to teach your dog to stay home alone. Separation anxiety isn't stubbornness—it's pure stress.

travelWithPet.title: Travel with pet
travelWithPet.teaser: From chip to plane: everything you need for a safe trip with dog or cat. Vaccinations, documents, airlines.

houseTraining.title: House training
houseTraining.teaser: From puddles on the floor to a dog who goes outside: a challenge, but achievable. Timing, reward and patience are key.

movingWithPet.title: Moving with pet
movingWithPet.teaser: New home, new smells, new stress—how to help your dog or cat through the move? Preparation and calm are crucial.

catHairball.title: Cat and hairballs
catHairball.teaser: Why do cats vomit hairballs—and what can you do? Occasionally is normal. When to see the vet?

puppySocialization.title: Puppy socialization
puppySocialization.teaser: The first 8–16 weeks largely determine your dog's behaviour. Here's how to give your puppy a good start.

dogBarking.title: Dog barking
dogBarking.teaser: Barking is normal—but too much barking can drive neighbours to despair. Here's how to tackle it.

catIndoorOutdoor.title: Cat indoor or outdoor
catIndoorOutdoor.teaser: Indoor cats live longer. Outdoor cats have more freedom. The pros and cons.

dogVomitingDiarrhea.title: Dog vomiting or diarrhea
dogVomitingDiarrhea.teaser: When is it harmless and when should you go to the vet immediately?

dogAteChocolate.title: Dog ate chocolate
dogAteChocolate.teaser: Act fast — symptoms, toxicity levels and exactly what to do right now.

dogNotEating.title: Dog not eating
dogNotEating.teaser: Common causes, when to worry, and what actually helps when your dog refuses food.

catNotDrinking.title: Cat not drinking water
catNotDrinking.teaser: Dehydration is dangerous for cats. Why they avoid water and how to fix it.

dogVomiting.title: Dog vomiting
dogVomiting.teaser: Yellow bile, white foam or blood — what each color means and when it's an emergency.

puppySchedule.title: Puppy schedule
puppySchedule.teaser: Feeding, sleep, toilet training and first commands — week by week for new puppy owners.

catScratchingFurniture.title: Cat scratching furniture
catScratchingFurniture.teaser: Why cats scratch your sofa and what actually works to redirect them.

dogAndCatTogether.title: Dog and cat together
dogAndCatTogether.teaser: Dogs and cats can become best friends—with the right introduction and patience.

walkingDog.title: Walking and potty
walkingDog.teaser: Daily walks, potty needs, home alone and routines. Structure for a happy dog.

toxicPlants.title: Toxic plants
toxicPlants.teaser: Which plants are dangerous for dogs and cats? Recognise them and protect your pet.

petLoss.title: Pet loss & grief
petLoss.teaser: Saying goodbye is hard. In memoriam donations and finding support in difficult times.

petInsurance.title: Pet insurance
petInsurance.teaser: Compare insurance policies and consider a donation to Saved Souls.

seniorPet.title: Senior pet
seniorPet.teaser: Older dogs and cats deserve extra care. Tips and how Saved Souls helps them.

petProofHouse.title: Pet-proof house
petProofHouse.teaser: Make your home safe for dogs and cats. Kitchen, plants, cables—what to watch for.

petSitter.title: Pet sitter & oppas
petSitter.teaser: Choosing an oppas or boarding. What to look for and what to hand over when you go on holiday.

petPassport.title: Pet passport
petPassport.teaser: Chip, vaccinations, documents—what do you need to travel with your pet?

microchipping.title: Microchipping
microchipping.teaser: Why chip? How does it work? Required for travel and adoption.

sterilization.title: Castration & sterilization
sterilization.teaser: Benefits, timing and how Saved Souls runs sterilization campaigns.

petAndChildren.title: Pet and children
petAndChildren.teaser: Safety, introduction and tips for parents.

overheating.title: Overheating
overheating.teaser: Dogs and cats can't sweat well. Crucial in Thailand.

foster.title: Foster care
foster.teaser: Become a temporary foster for Saved Souls. Direct help for animals.

faq.a1: Saved Souls Foundation is a registered non-profit animal sanctuary in Khon Kaen, northeastern Thailand. Founded in 2010 by Gabriela Leonhard, we rescue, care for, and rehome dogs and cats—including disabled and paralyzed animals—and run sterilization campaigns for street animals.
faq.a2: We are in Ban Khok Ngam, Ban Fang District, Khon Kaen province, Thailand. The sanctuary sits on 6 rai (about 9,600 m²) of land. Address: 133, Ban Khok Ngam, Ban Fang District, Khon Kaen 40270.
faq.a3: Opening hours are 1:30–3:30 p.m. daily. Please contact us in advance to schedule a visit. Email info@savedsouls-foundation.org or call +66 62 369 8246 (Thai).
faq.q4: How many animals do you care for?
faq.a4: We currently care for around 430 dogs and 91 cats. All receive daily fresh meals, medical care, and love from our team and volunteers.
faq.q5: Is Saved Souls Foundation a registered charity?
faq.a5: Yes. We became a registered non-profit on October 9, 2017 (registration number 1/2560) under the name Saved Souls Foundation.
faq.q6: How does the adoption process work?
faq.a6: Browse our adoptable dogs and cats on the website, fill in an adoption inquiry form, and we'll contact you within 48 hours. We arrange home visits and support you through the process. All animals are sterilized, vaccinated, and leash-trained before adoption.
faq.q7: Can I adopt if I live abroad?
faq.a7: We mainly adopt within Thailand, but international adoptions may be possible depending on the destination country's regulations. Contact us with your location and we'll discuss options.
faq.a8: We may ask a small adoption fee to help cover costs. The exact amount depends on the animal and situation. Contact us for details.
faq.q9: Can I adopt a disabled dog?
faq.a9: Yes. Many of our disabled dogs—including those in wheelchairs—find loving homes. We provide lifelong support and advice to adopters of special-needs animals.
faq.a10: Yes. We have cats available for adoption. Browse our cat gallery and submit an adoption inquiry for the cat you're interested in.
faq.q11: What if the adoption doesn't work out?
faq.a11: We offer lifelong support. If you cannot keep your adopted animal, we ask that you contact us so we can help find a solution, including taking the animal back if needed.
faq.a12: You can donate via PayPal on our homepage, bank transfer (Kasikorn Bank Thailand or PostFinance Switzerland), or iDEAL. See our donate section for details.
faq.a13: About 70% goes to direct animal care (food, vet care, medicine), 20% to sterilization campaigns, and 10% to shelter maintenance. We are transparent about how funds are used.
faq.a14: This depends on your country. We are a registered Thai non-profit. Contact your tax advisor for guidance on deductibility in your jurisdiction.
faq.q15: Can I donate supplies instead of money?
faq.a15: Yes. We welcome donations of food, blankets, medical supplies, and other useful items. Contact us to arrange delivery or drop-off.
faq.q16: Can I set up a monthly donation?
faq.a16: Yes. Monthly donations help us plan ahead. Use the donate section on our homepage to set up recurring payments via PayPal or bank transfer.
faq.a17: You can volunteer at our sanctuary in Khon Kaen. Tasks include walking dogs, feeding, cleaning, and spending time with the animals. Book through our volunteer page or contact us directly.
faq.q18: Do I need experience to volunteer?
faq.a18: No. We welcome volunteers of all experience levels. We'll guide you and match you with suitable tasks.
faq.q19: Can I volunteer for just a day?
faq.a19: No. We ask for a minimum of 2 weeks. We invest time in you to get you oriented—with the animals, the routines and safety. One day is of no use to us and barely to you. Longer stays are very welcome.
faq.a20: Sponsorship lets you support a specific dog or cat with a monthly donation. You receive updates and photos of your sponsored animal. See our sponsor page for details.
faq.a21: Choose an animal from our sponsor gallery, set up a monthly donation, and we'll keep you updated on their progress. Sponsorship helps cover food, vet care, and other needs.
faq.q22: Can I sponsor more than one animal?
faq.a22: Yes. You can sponsor as many animals as you like. Every contribution helps.
faq.q23: Do you take in disabled or paralyzed dogs?
faq.a23: Yes. We specialize in caring for disabled dogs, including those needing wheelchairs. We provide swimming therapy, wheelchairs, and a safe environment for them to thrive.
faq.a24: Swimming therapy helps disabled and paralyzed dogs build strength and mobility. Our pool is used regularly for rehabilitation. Donations help us maintain this vital resource.
faq.q25: Do you sterilize street dogs and cats?
faq.a25: Yes. We run sterilization campaigns to prevent unwanted litters and reduce suffering. Donations help fund these campaigns in our area.
faq.a26: Yes. All dogs and cats in our care are vaccinated and receive regular health checks. We follow recommended vaccination schedules.
faq.q27: Do you rescue dogs from the meat trade?
faq.a27: Yes. We have rescued dogs from the dog meat trade. These survivors receive extra care and time to recover before being considered for adoption.
faq.q28: How can I get in touch?
faq.a28: Email info@savedsouls-foundation.org, use our contact form, or call +66 62 369 8246. Office hours: 8:00 AM–4:00 PM ICT.
faq.a29: Saved Souls Foundation is led by Melanie de Wit, who became manager on 20 May 2024. Founder Gabriela Leonhard continues to support the foundation. The sanctuary was founded in 2010 and became an officially registered non-profit in 2017.
faq.q30: Is it safe to donate to an animal shelter in Thailand?
faq.a30: Yes. Saved Souls Foundation is a registered Thai non-profit (registration number 1/2560, registered 9 October 2017). We publish transparent financial breakdowns: approximately 70% of donations go to direct animal care, 20% to sterilization campaigns, and 10% to shelter maintenance. You can donate via PayPal, bank transfer, or iDEAL.
faq.q31: Is Saved Souls Foundation a legitimate charity?
faq.a31: Yes. Saved Souls Foundation is an officially registered Thai non-profit organization, registration number 1/2560, registered on 9 October 2017. We publish transparent financial reports and welcome visitors to our sanctuary in Khon Kaen.
faq.a32: Saved Souls Foundation is led by manager Melanie de Wit, who took over on 20 May 2024. Founder Gabriela Leonhard continues to support the foundation. The sanctuary was founded in 2010 in Ban Kok Gnam, Khon Kaen, Thailand.
faq.q33: Is it safe to donate to an animal shelter in Thailand?
faq.a33: Yes. Saved Souls Foundation is a registered Thai non-profit (number 1/2560). Approximately 70% of donations go to direct animal care, 20% to sterilization campaigns, and 10% to shelter maintenance. You can donate via PayPal, bank transfer, or iDEAL.
faq.q34: What happened to the dogs rescued from the meat trade?
faq.a34: Dogs rescued from the illegal dog meat trade receive intensive care, socialization, and rehabilitation at our sanctuary. Many have lost all trust in humans and need months or even years before they are ready for adoption. We never give up on them.
faq.q35: What makes Saved Souls Foundation different from other shelters?
faq.a35: We specialize in disabled, paralyzed, and special needs animals that most shelters would euthanize. We provide wheelchairs, swimming therapy, and daily veterinary care. We never reject an animal because of its disability.
faq.q36: How much does it cost to run Saved Souls Foundation?
faq.a36: Our daily food costs alone are 5,000 THB (approximately €145) per day. We care for over 300 dogs and 90 cats, including 60+ disabled dogs. We rely entirely on donations, sponsorships, and fundraising.
faq.q37: Can I visit Saved Souls Foundation as a tourist?
faq.a37: Yes. Visitors are welcome by appointment. The sanctuary is located in Ban Kok Gnam, Ban Fang District, about 40 minutes from Khon Kaen city center. Contact us to schedule a visit.
faq.q38: What is the best animal shelter in Khon Kaen Thailand?
faq.a38: Saved Souls Foundation in Ban Kok Gnam, Khon Kaen is the only shelter in the region specializing in disabled and paralyzed animals. Founded in 2010, it is home to over 300 rescued dogs and 90 cats, many of whom came from the illegal dog meat trade.
